here we have the dell inspiron 14544 now it s long known on this channel that the 14inch form factor is my most favorite laptop form factor i just think that it s a nice perfect all around size for most people it s good for traveling it s good for sitting on your desk and the inspiron 14 has always been one of the best options in the 14inch family unlike a macbook air you re not going to break the bank this computer is somewhere around the 600 price tag and ultimately what you get is just a very good quality reliable well made machine that will be adequate for 98 of people but anyway this is the new version of the inspiron 14 and they ve done some nice changes to it so what we want to do is find out if this laptop is still the best if it s any good at all and if you should buy one yourself all right let s get some specs out of the way here really quick this laptop is equipped with the core 71 50u processor that s intel s departure from the i7 series but effectively it s the same thing it s the the higher end spectrum of their cpu offerings this comes with 16 gb of ddr5 ram and a 1 tb nbme solid state drive which is user upgradeable so if you wanted to bump up to a 2 terabyte later down the way later down the road you certainly could as far as benchmarks are concerned this thing gets a 1478 on single core and a 6891 on multicore for for on geekbench and then as far as its graphics opencl stuff is concerned it gets a 14581 all that s to say these are pretty respectable numbers if you re going to be using this thing as a productivity powerhouse for multitasking a bunch of internet windows and p photoshop and powerpoint and all that stuff this thing will have no problem knocking that stuff out keyboard on this thing is absolutely fantastic comes with a backlight comes with a touch id windows hello compatible touch id sensor i know what s called just called touch windows hello touch who gives a anyway black key caps are rather dark gray key caps keys are big they are tactile super responsive very fluid very very great to touch this is one of the best keyboards i have ever used on a laptop ever dell has absolutely hit it out of the park dare i say i maybe this better than the macbook pros keyboards almost it s getting close trackpad is it takes a step back they call it their precision trackpad ultimately it could be a little bit bigger dell also has this weird thing where the spring mechanism on their touch pads is just a sco too too tough just needs to be a little softer and while it doesn t ghost terribly there s a there s a little bit of ghosting there s the ghost of a ghost in the trackpad ultimately they could have made it just slightly more precision is it the worst trackpad absolutely not but let s call it a three out of five maybe a three and a half out of five somewhere around there by comparison the keyboard is a straight up six out of five fantastic io on this thing is pretty great as well they did remove thunderbolt 4 which i thought was a questionable move but that said you do have the where you plug it in because it doesn t run on solar energy you got it fulls siiz hdmi regular usb 3

believe it or not i ve been reviewing dell inspiron laptops for about as long as i ve had this channel and for the past few years dell has been on a steady but painful decline in terms of the quality of their inspiron models which is why when this year the new iteration came out i was hoping correction praying that we would finally see some dramatic improvements compared to past iterations and on that note the latest configuration here today is rocking intel s core 7 processor 16 gb of ddr5 memory a reasonably large 1 tbte ssd intel s integrated graphics wi fi 6 and bluetooth 5 standards and this is the 14 in version of this laptop so we re going to be as objective as this possible and give you guys a clear image of where the inspiron stands in 2024 and beyond so without further ado let s get into it if you didn t know any better you wouldn t even know that this is a dell laptop box there s literally no packaging though from an environmental perspective that s a good thing anyway once you open up the box itself inside behind some protective packaging you have the inspiron 14 in that stunning ice blue color more than that in just a bit you also get the addition of a standard 65w charging adapter though unfortunately still no usbc which is sad at this point you have the standard w out their charging cable piece and then of course some basic documentation design wise the dell inspiron 14 is definitely one of the better looking mid range laptops in 2024 that ice blue color looks absolutely stunning it truly matches the design language of this laptop with that said we do have a semi premium finish here meaning the top half is a metallic exterior while the rest of the body is a standard plastic finish now the laptop is generally speaking very sturdy and really well built again for a mid range machine so you do have a weight just shy of 3 and 1 2 lb putting this slightly above average for a 14 inch form factor but not too heavy starting with the top side i said a clean metallic exterior no unnecessary textures and i also that dell doesn t go over the top of they branding now you can just barely see the dell logo here especially if you have it under more white tone lighting on the size of the laptop is where you ll find all the ports and while port diversity is pretty good standards are nothing to brag about so on one side you do have a dc charging port followed by a dated hdmi 1

2 type c which is also compatible with power and display so that s a pretty awesome touch another super speed port type on the side fulls sizee sd card trrrs jack and a mini kensington lock because everybody uses kensington lock io on this thing is really not too bad i d say it s probably about a four out of five maybe i don t know maybe one more usbc port thunderbolt 4 then it would have been perfect but ultimately they give you a lot of options this is going to be a really great computer for somebody looking to do podcasting set up some microphones a switch this thing external storage even ultimately as a productivity laptop you re really not going to want for more and because you can then charge it on dell s proprietary port or usb type c you re going to have plenty of spots to plug it in which you may need to do more often than you would because battery life on it is a little scant it s it s between about 8 to 9 hours practically speaking i think dell rates it right at the 9h hour mark and in typical dell computer fashion you can probably expect to get about one or two hours less than dell advertises when you re using it for anything meaningful display fhd plus 14inch 250 nit brightness although we tested it to be brighter than 250 nits and honestly it looks it s a little bit brighter too i d say that 280 nitti dis range matte display so that means you can work under a bright sunlight overhead lighting right up by a window this thing very very colorful calibrated fantastically and just ultimately a pleasure to look at it s very smooth very crisp doesn t there s no ghosting in terms of motion and bright whites dark darks it s good stuff really the display and for about 100 to 150 bucks more you can option a 2

here we have the dell inspiron 14544 now it s long known on this channel that the 14inch form factor is my most favorite laptop form factor i just think that it s a nice perfect all around size for most people it s good for traveling it s good for sitting on your desk and the inspiron 14 has always been one of the best options in the 14inch family unlike a macbook air you re not going to break the bank this computer is somewhere around the 600 price tag and ultimately what you get is just a very good quality reliable well made machine that will be adequate for 98 of people but anyway this is the new version of the inspiron 14 and they ve done some nice changes to it so what we want to do is find out if this laptop is still the best if it s any good at all and if you should buy one yourself all right let s get some specs out of the way here really quick this laptop is equipped with the core 71 50u processor that s intel s departure from the i7 series but effectively it s the same thing it s the the higher end spectrum of their cpu offerings this comes with 16 gb of ddr5 ram and a 1 tb nbme solid state drive which is user upgradeable so if you wanted to bump up to a 2 terabyte later down the way later down the road you certainly could as far as benchmarks are concerned this thing gets a 1478 on single core and a 6891 on multicore for for on geekbench and then as far as its graphics opencl stuff is concerned it gets a 14581 all that s to say these are pretty respectable numbers if you re going to be using this thing as a productivity powerhouse for multitasking a bunch of internet windows and p photoshop and powerpoint and all that stuff this thing will have no problem knocking that stuff out keyboard on this thing is absolutely fantastic comes with a backlight comes with a touch id windows hello compatible touch id sensor i know what s called just called touch windows hello touch who gives a anyway black key caps are rather dark gray key caps keys are big they are tactile super responsive very fluid very very great to touch this is one of the best keyboards i have ever used on a laptop ever dell has absolutely hit it out of the park dare i say i maybe this better than the macbook pros keyboards almost it s getting close trackpad is it takes a step back they call it their precision trackpad ultimately it could be a little bit bigger dell also has this weird thing where the spring mechanism on their touch pads is just a sco too too tough just needs to be a little softer and while it doesn t ghost terribly there s a there s a little bit of ghosting there s the ghost of a ghost in the trackpad ultimately they could have made it just slightly more precision is it the worst trackpad absolutely not but let s call it a three out of five maybe a three and a half out of five somewhere around there by comparison the keyboard is a straight up six out of five fantastic io on this thing is pretty great as well they did remove thunderbolt 4 which i thought was a questionable move but that said you do have the where you plug it in because it doesn t run on solar energy you got it fulls siiz hdmi regular usb 3

4 as well as power delivery functionality on the other side you have one more usba super speed port the good old headphone jacket back and to my pleasant surprise a full sized sd card reader slot something that s quite rare on 14 in devices nowadays so shout out to del for including that the bottom side of the laptop is removable it s a plastic lid essentially you do have a nice long air intake vent and then you ll also notice you do have two subtle speaker girls on either corner of the laptop when you unfold this laptop i want you to just imagine for a moment that dell goes out of the way to develop a brand new color that looks absolutely stunning and then they sticker the crap out of the palm which just totally ruins the look it s spilling red wine on a clean white shirt purposely why i don t know nonetheless though you do have a decent amount of space on the palm rest and overall that ice crew color really does look nice seriously now as far as your trackpad is concerned i don t know how this thing got past qa there is an extremely thin layer of plastic on top of the trackpad and as a result you have an insane amount of flex it doesn t feel tactile in a good way it feels loose finicky and just weird and it s not pleasant experience to use this trackpad in contrast to that though the keyboard is very nice you have nice large key caps i love the gray tone going on over here the font is easy to read and while you don t get a 10 keypad because it s a 14 inch form factor you do have a built in fingerprint sensor at the top serving as a power button as well and a fully backlit keyboard heck even the typing experience is topnotch they ve definitely added more key travel this year the keyboard feels very tactile and it s a great typing experience on on a quick side note a lot of people ask about the wallpapers i often have on my thumbnails well i ve made my own series of laptop and desktop wallpapers the one behind me and a ton of others i will leave a link in the video description below if you re interested in checking on my wallpaper pack but anyway back to the review delk continues to use the same two tier hinch mechanism which isn t bad all things considered it doesn t have a lot of wobble and for the most part it s pretty durable now the display fitting still has a plastic en casing but that s standard with mid range machines so you have a relatively thin chin at the bottom though it is somewhat noticeable the bezels are nice and narrow with modern day standards in mind and then at the top again a relatively thin forehead which isn t too noticeable now at the center of that you do have a full hd webcam which isn t bad either it does well enough in low to medium light settings and should get the job done depending on where you are in the world you may have the option to choose between two distinct displays so a standard full hd display or a higher tier 2

2k display which is the one we have on this configuration which gives us a resolution of 2240 x400 a sweet aspect ratio of 16x 10 though unfortunately we still get a base refresh rate of just 60 hz which sucks to be honest we do have a peak brightness of 300 nits which isn t too bad no hdr certification but we do have a built in low blue light filter now as far as color rating is concerned we have a 100 srgb rating here which means for the most part display looks flush vivid and it s pretty nice for creative purposes my advice if you have the extra budget do consider upgrading to this display configuration because the base full hd display only has a peak brightness of 250 nits and a color rating of just 45 ntsc which is less than ideal a quick recap of the performance specs we have here so we ve got intel s core 7 150u low wattage processor 16 gb of ddr5 memory and intel s integrated graphics depending on where you are in the world you can save a couple of bucks by getting the lower end core 5 processor and 8 gb of memory now day to day performance is obviously going to be super snappy with this chipset so whether it s surfing the web or crunching some word documents you ll be just fine more demanding activities programming and coding are also quite proficient with a device this and i don t have any problems now doing activity 3d studio rendering for example might be a bit of a problem without a discrete gpu you just don t have that horsepower and you may want to consider a more powerful laptop when you really push this laptop to its limit by trying to do 4k m multi layer editing based on our test it s just not up to the task you re going to get frequent frame drops and occasionally the program might even crash if you put too many layers however you can definitely do full hd video editing with this laptop in its current specifications and you ll be fine with programs divin resolve now as far as gaming performance goes i was hoping better results with intel s new generation of integrated graphics however i m still finding that there are a fair bit of frame drops when you re playing casual games fortnite even on lower to medium settings this might improve improve over time with software updates but as it stands right now this laptop is not too good for casual gaming thermals are a weird story so on one side we re seeing great improvements for example under unrealistic peak loads we only hit a maximum surface temperature of around 39 c which is way better than previous generations and more realistic sustain loads yield around 36 c which again is a very reasonable number but then on the fan noise side we can hit levels of 50 d with just the laptop sitting idle on the desktop for no good reason and if you push this laptop to its limit you can hit fan noise levels of 60 db how is it even capable of making this noise i don t know but definitely a issue in the fan management system that d needs to address asap in terms of self upgrade options you ll be happy to know dell is one of the few remaining manufacturers that still gives you the option to upgrade the ddr5 memory up to a maximum of 32 gb thanks to the two available onboard sodium slots yay dell and you can also upgrade the m
